# civ6-mcp

> An MCP server that connects LLM agents to Civilization VI for strategic reasoning evaluation.

## About

civ6-mcp is an open-source environment that enables language model agents to play full games of Civilization VI through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). A single episode spans 330 turns across seven concurrent strategic domains, producing thousands of tool invocations.

## Key Pages

- [Documentation](/docs): Technical documentation, architecture, and API reference
- [CivBench](/civbench): Benchmark scenarios for evaluating strategic reasoning
- [Games](/games): Browse game sessions with per-turn telemetry
- [About](/about): Project overview and methodology

## Tools

The environment exposes 77 MCP tools covering units, cities, diplomacy, research, governance, religion, espionage, trade, World Congress, and game lifecycle.

- [Tool Reference: Queries](/docs/tools/query): State observation tools
- [Tool Reference: Actions](/docs/tools/actions): Game action tools
- [Tool Reference: System](/docs/tools/system): Lifecycle and management tools

## Research

The environment identifies the *sensorium effect*: agents receive the same facts a human sees, but cannot replicate passive peripheral attention. Information not explicitly queried does not enter the agent's world model.

## Source Code

- GitHub: https://github.com/lmwilki/civ6-mcp
- License: MIT
